name: ASW
bio: Seattle-based DJ and electronic music collective active in the PNW underground scene. Runs the ASW Mix Series, featuring underground electronic artists from around the world. Active in the Shameless community and related Seattle events.

name: Abraham
bio: Abraham is a Seattle DJ documented on the Studio 4/4 stage at Shameless' 7th Memorial Day Hijinks at Monkey Loft. That 2022 lineup placed him alongside Black Velveteen, Jesse Leer, Simone BG, and Snapdragon, with Desert Hearts on the rooftop and Shameless residents in the loft. Abraham lands as local support from the house and techno side of the Seattle circuit, connected to Studio 4/4 and made for daytime rooftop momentum.

name: Acid Tourist
bio: Acid Tourist is Holmar, formerly of Thugfucker, bringing a seasoned, psychedelic approach to house and techno. His work under the Acid Tourist name keeps the focus on movement, atmosphere, and the loose edges where club music gets strange. Seattle lineups have placed him in rooms connected to long-running house culture, including Flammable and Uniting Souls circles. Acid Tourist brings road-worn, trippy club energy built for dancers who stay late.

name: Adlib
bio: Adlib is Devin Kelly, the Seattle DJ, producer, and promoter whose work with Shameless and People of Earth has helped keep the city's underground mischievous and moving. A self-taught DJ since 1998, he came up through Idaho, Boise, and Salt Lake City scenes before becoming a regular force across Western clubs, renegades, and outdoor gatherings. His sets are known for technical blends, darker progressive pressure, acid, techno, and enough crowd-rocking instinct to keep the heady stuff from floating away. Adlib is pure Shameless DNA: radical good times, serious tunes, and dance-floor debauchery with a working brain.

name: Aivilo
bio: Digital/analog DJ known for deep/minimal house, techno, breaks and acid. Part of Shameless crew since \~2013.

name: Alessandro Carrabba
bio: Seattle-based DJ and co-founder of OFF99 alongside Keenan Stevenson. Their West Coast house project draws from soul, disco, jazz, and hip-hop influences. OFF99 has hosted legends including Omar S, Fred P, DJ QU, and Palms Trax, and launched the HouseOFF99 mix series spotlighting Pacific Northwest DJs. A regular presence at Kremwerk and Monkey Loft.

name: Alex Flores
bio: PNW DJ and promoter active since 2010. A steady presence in Seattle's underground dance music scene, Alex advocates for techno, industrial, and darker 4/4 sounds. Co-founder and promoter with Depth & Macabre events in Seattle. Plays regularly at Kremwerk and other Seattle underground venues.

name: Alex the Cosmic Therapist
bio: Alex the Cosmic Therapist is a Seattle DJ/live artist appearing in Shameless-adjacent community listings, including Breakfast Club with Wajatta, DJ Heather, The Fungineers, B.Fly, Black Velveteen, Riz, Recess, Tek Jones, Shannon O, WD4D, and other Northwest fixtures. Additional RA listings place Alex in the Grooving for Good resident circle, where the programming leans hip-hop, pop, funk, mid-tempo, downtempo, alternative, and electronic music. Alex the Cosmic Therapist fits as an eclectic connector: playful, healing-room energy with enough rhythm to land in a club setting, and a name that signals mood as much as genre.

name: Alexia.F
bio: Seattle-native DJ with a deep love for minimal beats and the underground electronic music scene. Part of the Between the Lines City Collective and a regular performer in both Seattle and Los Angeles. Known for drawing out the emotion in minimal and underground sounds.

name: Alfonso Tan
bio: Seattle-based DJ/producer specializing in house and adjacent dance music. Plays at Monkey Loft, LOST, Uniting Souls, Studio 4/4.

name: Alice Camille
bio: Seattle-based DJ immersed in the electronic music scene since the late 1990s. A thoughtful selector with a wealth of experience in underground dance music. Regular performer at Kremwerk, Monkey Loft, and other Pacific Northwest venues, often playing b2b with Audie Dimitri.

name: Alison Scott
bio: Seattle-based certified yoga instructor and community figure, a regular at Shameless Productions events. Featured at Reverie Society daytime parties at Monkey Loft, co-presented by Shameless, Uniting Souls, and Viva Recordings. Leads live yoga sessions alongside electronic music DJs.

name: Almond Brown
bio: Seattle DJ and member of the Board of Deacons alongside Riz Rollins, Devlin Jenkins, and Joey Webb. Active in Seattle's underground house music community.

name: Ammo
bio: Seattle-based DJ and live performer bringing dynamic sets to the underground dance scene. A regular presence in the city's electronic music community with an energetic, crowd-focused approach.

name: Ana-Lucid
bio: Ana-Lucid is Ana-Lisa, a Pacific Northwest producer and DJ shaping minimal beats, ethereal soundscapes, driving basslines, and deep grooves. Her public profiles connect her to Restless Planet, Makes Me Human, Flammable, Space Yacht, Between The Lines, Monkey Loft, Substation, and Kremwerk orbit. Since beginning to curate her sound in 2020, she has moved toward groovy, whimsical, spiritually tuned club music with a real local footprint.

name: Anabel Englund
bio: Anabel Englund is a Southern California singer, songwriter, producer, and DJ whose voice helped define a polished, emotionally direct corner of modern house music. Her collaborations with Hot Natured, MK, Lee Foss, Jamie Jones, and Hot Creations turned into major club moments, while later work on AREA10, Ultra, Defected, and her debut album Messing with Magic pushed her further into dance-pop territory. She carries hooks like a pop writer but understands the physical payoff of a house record. Anabel brings crossover glamour with club credibility,big vocals, real groove, and no shortage of lift.

name: Anakim
bio: Anakim is Riccardo LeBron, a Los Angeles-based DJ and producer whose own phrase for the sound is "Intergalactic Underground Sounds." His melodic house and techno leans cinematic, futuristic, dark, and emotionally charged, with concept-driven releases and a style shaped by Icon Collective, LA club culture, and big-room underground stages. The music is polished but not soft, often building suspense before opening into something huge. Anakim brings sci-fi drama, melodic pressure, and a little cosmic menace.

name: Andromo
bio: Vancouver-based DJ and producer originally from El Salvador, known for blending lush melodies with driving percussion for an emotive dance floor experience. Owner of Rainforest Music label. An active presence in the Pacific Northwest underground electronic music circuit.

name: Andy Warren
bio: Andy Warren is a Portland-based DJ and co-founder of Believe You Me and Process PDX, two names tied to the Pacific Northwest's deeper underground dance network. His work as a promoter and selector has helped build rooms where house, techno, and left-of-center club music can breathe outside the obvious circuit. As a DJ, he brings the patience of someone who understands both sides of the booth: the architecture of the night and the records that make it worth staying. Andy brings Portland's understated depth,smart pacing, durable grooves, and no unnecessary gloss.

name: Animyx
bio: Seattle-based DJ known for a nostalgic, daytime-dancing flavor blending house and classic sounds. A regular at Monkey Loft and part of the Restless Planet crew, bringing warm, soul-driven sets to the Pacific Northwest underground scene.

name: Aos
bio: Aos is a Seattle producer, DJ, organizer, and member of secondnature and TUF. Her background runs through Tacoma DIY spaces, KUPS college radio, queer community work, and the textured edge of the Northwest underground. The sound is slippery and emotional: rhythmic enough for the floor, strange enough to keep the room awake. In Shameless language, Aos is sunrise music with teeth.

name: Area 303
bio: Area 303 is a Seattle live techno and acid project associated with Larry Kleinke and Wilf Libgott, built around hardware-driven performance rather than laptop polish. Press around their live setup points to drum machines, acid lines, and improvisation. Area 303 brings the classic machine-room feeling: raw sequences, squelch, and Seattle techno with its sleeves rolled up.

name: Aria Bare
bio: Aria Bare is a queer techno producer and DJ rooted in the Pacific Northwest underground. Their music favors atmosphere, hardware-minded texture, and the kind of shadowy momentum that works as well in a DIY room as it does in a late-night club slot. As a selector, Aria Bare keeps the focus on tension and release rather than obvious shortcuts, letting experimental instincts sit inside functional techno pressure. The set comes with teeth and atmosphere: intimate, physical, and tuned for dancers who like the edges left sharp.

name: Ashley Younnia (LA)
bio: Ashley Younniä is a Los Angeles-based DJ, curator, and performing artist with a sound rooted in Black, queer, and femme-led house culture. Her work with parties like Stardust and TheyHouseLA has helped shape a soulful, sweaty corner of LA's underground. She plays with groove first, keeping the room lifted without sanding down the edges. A Shameless set from Ashley lands in that sweet spot between deep feeling, body music, and full dance-floor release.

name: Atlee
bio: Seattle DJ (Atlee Treasure) who has been active in the city's nightclub and radio scene since the early 2000s. Co-founded the Trust party series in 2009 alongside Kid Hops and Jason Justice. Owner of the Momentos Records label and music project Rozalesdmusic.

name: Audie Dimitri
bio: Audie Dimitri is a Seattle-based DJ known for all-vinyl dance parties, deep house, and underground club sounds. She has appeared on Innerflight Broadcast and plays local rooms including The Rendezvous and Monkey Loft. She often plays back-to-back with Alice Camille at Shameless and Reverie Society events.

name: Aundrea
bio: Seattle-based DJ bringing soulful and rhythmic sounds to the underground dance scene. A presence at Monkey Loft and other Seattle venues, playing alongside the city's finest DJs for 1luv events and beyond.

name: Aurora Flux
bio: Seattle-based DJ delivering energetic, genre-crossing sets in the underground electronic music scene. A regular presence at local events, bringing powerful dance floor energy and a dynamic range of electronic sounds.

name: Austin Robertson
bio: Seattle-via-SoCal DJ and event promoter, founder of Routine,a monthly event series at Ballard's Substation venue. Active in the Seattle underground electronic music scene.

name: Avignon
bio: Seattle-based DJ, producer, and owner of AVIGNON ENTERTAINMENT (real name Michael Robertson). Influenced by DJ SNEAK, Low Steppa, OFFAIAH, and Franky Rizardo. A featured guest on C89.5 Seattle Summer Sessions and a regular presence at Seattle underground events and Shameless parties.

name: B.Fly
bio: Seattle DJ Jyoti B.Fly, 19-year DJ veteran and KEXP radio host of Wo'Pop world music show. Caravan Seattle resident known for global house, breaks, and funk.

name: B.LakePeterson
bio: B.LakePeterson is a Seattle DJ active across community-driven dance spaces, from Belltown Art Walk sets to Monkey Loft lineups and Shameless-linked events. Listings connect the name to Reverie Society's Cascadia Winter Solstice closing party, Deck'd Out season-closure programming, and Breakfast Club. The sound lands in a social, room-aware lane built for art spaces, daytime dance floors, and late-night house and techno gatherings.

name: BIG SIR
bio: Seattle-based DJ (Casey Berner) delivering tech house and deep house to the city's queer underground and club scene. A regular at The Cuff Complex, Monkey Loft, and Reverie Society events. Plays everything from deep house to EDM and melodic vibes.

name: Bad Ginger
bio: Seattle DJ known for sultry deep house and dirty, high-energy heaters. A fixture in Seattle's local lady DJ scene.

name: Banyan
bio: Seattle-based DJ bringing organic, natural-sounding electronic music to the underground dance scene. Known for lush, layered sounds and an immersive approach to dance floor storytelling.

name: Ben Annand
bio: West Coast festival DJ with deep house to techno roots going back to 1995. A top West Coast festival performer with credits at Lightning in a Bottle, Desert Hearts, Shambhala, Oregon Eclipse, and Photosynthesis. Runs B2P booking agency, placing acts across the West Coast from San Diego to Seattle.

name: Best Butt Camp
bio: Seattle-based DJ camp and event collective founded in 2016. Known for throwing booty-forward, body-positive dance parties at venues like Nectar Lounge. Affiliated with DJ Cheeks and Neon Lounge NW. A fun and inclusive presence in the Seattle underground dance scene.

name: Bex
bio: Chicago-born DJ now based in Seattle as a resident. A regular at Shameless Productions' Deck'd Out summer series and vinyl sets at Monkey Loft. Known for a diverse, free-flowing style that spans house, live-streamed sets, and intimate club performances across Seattle.

name: Biplov
bio: Seattle-based Nepalese-American DJ and co-founder of Caravan Seattle. One half of the Silk Road DJ duo alongside Yelda (an Afghan-American DJ), sharing global beats that span world music, electronic, and beyond. A community builder in Seattle's diverse dance music scene.

name: Bizio
bio: Bizio is Nico Bisio, a Portland-based DJ and promoter active in Oregon's underground electronic community. His Healing House Music identity points toward a broad, psychedelic lane where house, techno, and communal dance culture meet without getting precious. As both organizer and selector, he understands the practical work behind a good room: sound, flow, trust, and the small decisions that let people open up. Bizio brings Northwest ritual energy,earthy, heady, and still fully committed to the kick drum.

name: Black Velveteen
bio: Black Velveteen (real name Chris Offutt) is a pillar of Seattle's dance music scene, originally from the South Side of Chicago. Navigating the underground scenes from Chicago to Seattle to San Francisco and back again, Offutt crafts dark, deep, and atmospheric electronic music with active releases on SoundCloud and an integrated show on Proton Radio. Their sound blends deep, ethereal, percussive, melodic, and sometimes dark elements, earning recognition as a creator, artist, writer, and DJ/producer in the Pacific Northwest.

name: Blk Lab
bio: New Jersey-born, Seattle-based DJ (John Labrador). Known for progressive house with Latin and tech house influences.

name: Blueyedsoul
bio: Blueyedsoul, also known as Big Room Benny or Benny Bitters, is a Seattle-based DJ bringing house music and big-room lift to the Pacific Northwest. A Capitol Hill Block Party regular with Neumos and Barboza ties, he plays for rooms that want familiar energy, local confidence, and a clean party read.

name: Bobi Stevkovski
bio: Atlanta-based DJ and producer. Creative Director at Satya Records. Co-founder of Zemya Fest.

name: Brian Lyons
bio: Brian Lyons is one of Seattle house music's steady hands, a Flammable founder and longtime champion of underground dance music. His sets carry the patience of someone who knows how to build a room instead of just hit it with peaks. Around Shameless, that makes him exactly the kind of selector who can turn a rooftop, basement, or late-night room into a proper Seattle house gathering. Deep grooves, warm pressure, and no wasted motion.

name: Brit
bio: Seattle DJ, producer, and electronic music figure for over two decades. Real name Brit Hansen. Co-founded the beloved duo Jacob London with Dave Pezzner, releasing funky underground tech-house 12-inches throughout the 2000s. Also active as a solo artist as Hanssen. Guest DJ for KEXP's Midnight in a Perfect World. Featured at Deck'd Out and Shameless events.

name: Brit Hansen
bio: Brit Hansen is a Seattle electronic music fixture whose work stretches from the beloved tech-house duo Jacob London to the left-field solo world of Hanssen. KEXP describes her as an integral figure in the local electronic community for more than two decades, and that history shows in the way she moves between weirdness and dance-floor function. Her solo direction leans cinematic, dreamy, and synth-heavy, while her DJ instincts still know exactly how to pull a room into hypnotic motion. Brit brings the rare combination of scene memory and restless curiosity,deep roots, strange colors, and zero autopilot.

name: Brooke Would
bio: Brooke Would is a Seattle selector connected to the funky side of underground house. Her SoundCloud trail under The Funky Kind points toward sets that favor groove, warmth, and the kind of low-key swing that makes a room loosen up without being pushed. The name reads playful, but the lane is practical: records that keep bodies moving and leave room for personality. Brooke Would brings the fun without flattening the taste.

name: Cami Jones
bio: Rooted in rhythm, soul, and pure dancefloor instinct, Cami Jones is a DJ, producer, and vocalist known for her deep, sultry fusion of house and techno—groove-heavy, emotionally charged, and impossible to ignore.Now based in Ibiza, Cami has become a trusted fixture on the island’s most iconic dancefloors, holding a residency at Pikes Ibiza, where her sets are known for their intimacy, unpredictability, and unforgettable energy. Whether warming up a terrace or commanding a peak-time room, she reads the crowd with precision—guiding dancers through raw, euphoric, and magnetic sonic journeys. Her music has been released on some of the most respected labels in underground dance music, including Hot Creations, Repopulate Mars, Kaluki, HE.SHE.THEY, and her own rising imprint, nooghty. As both a producer and vocalist, Cami weaves hypnotic textures and sensual toplines that blur genre boundaries and captivate dancefloors worldwide. What truly sets her apart is the emotional depth she brings to every performance. From Australia’s coastlines to the heart of the European circuit, Cami has built a reputation for crafting experiences—not just playing tracks. Her presence behind the decks is powerful, authentic, and refreshingly unpretentious. With a growing global following and a wave of new music and standout shows ahead in 2025, Cami Jones is already recognized as one of the most exciting and soulful voices in underground dance music—a talent defined by substance, groove, and timeless style.

name: Chaotic Noise Marching Corps
bio: Seattle renegade brass band dedicated to keeping the tradition of music, performance, energy, and sheer fun alive in the streets. Known for high-decibel immersive street shows.

name: Cheeks
bio: Cheeks is a Seattle-based DJ deeply embedded in the Shameless PNW and underground festival community. A regular at Monkey Loft's Deck'd Out series and Shameless Memorial Day Hijinks events alongside Desert Hearts DJs, Cheeks brings high-energy, eclectic sounds described as booty afro latin funked up acid house beats. Also affiliated with Best Butt Camp, a Burning Man theme camp, and part of the tight-knit Pacific Northwest queer underground scene.

name: Cherry White
bio: Seattle DJ with South African roots. Debuted in Seattle in 2018, blending afro house, deep house, and acid jazz. Has played Shameless Deck'd Out at Monkey Loft.

name: Chris Tower
bio: Chris Tower is a Seattle-based DJ, producer, and Opulent Temple regular with a sound built around progressive house, tech house, melodic techno, and sun-baked festival lift. Since starting behind the decks in 2009, he has become a steady presence in Seattle's underground and Burning Man-connected communities, including years producing Seattle Opulent Temple fundraisers. His productions, mixes, and playa-informed sets aim for scale without losing warmth: big melodies, rolling low end, and enough emotional pull to keep the room open. Chris Tower brings altitude,high-energy house music with community roots and a clear sense of occasion.

name: Christian Martin
bio: San Francisco-based DJ and founder of Trippy Ass Technologies. Known for house, techno, and DnB. Has released music on Dirtybird, Get Physical, Crosstown Rebels, and more.

name: Christine Michelle
bio: Seattle-based DJ and producer, born in Capitol Hill, crafting bass-forward, dark melodic, atmospheric soundscapes with world music textures.

name: Claudia LB
bio: Claudia LB is a Seattle-based queer Indigenous DJ and the force behind Fricktion Collective and Club Milk. Her sound pushes hypnotic, groove-driven techno, trance, club, and bouncy Eurodance, blending dark rolling low end with trance-washed melody and forward-moving rhythm. That combination gives her sets a specific charge: emotional, locked-in, and physical without defaulting to obvious peak-time formulas. Claudia LB brings new-generation Seattle underground energy with purpose, pressure, and a clear point of view.

name: Codebase
bio: Seattle-based DJ and producer (real name Tom Butcher). Codebase is his primary solo electronic project, focused on dance-oriented tracks. Active in the Shameless and PNW underground community; also performed at the Deck'd Out rooftop series at Monkey Loft.

name: Copeland
bio: Copeland (Erika) discovered Seattle's underground electronic music scene in 2008, becoming a devoted patron of the dance floor before transitioning to DJing in 2021. A Vitala Recordings resident and promoter, she has performed at festivals including Sun Worship, Afterglow, Cascadia, and Panacea, as well as events for the Dusty Booty Ranch and Fireburn, Flammable, Uniting Souls, and Studio 4/4. Known for her deep connection to the music community, she intuitively crafts sets spanning techno and deep house across Seattle venues like Monkey Loft, Madame Lou's, and Cherry.

name: Curses
bio: Berlin-based musician and DJ Luca Venezia, blending acid, post-punk, and dark disco. Latest album Another Heaven on Italians Do It Better. Performed at Shameless 23 Year Anniversary.

name: DJ Anzo
bio: Seattle-based DJ, co-host of the Jola Madi Podcast, and organizer behind Curated Vibez and Free Agent Seattle. An active community member in Seattle's dance music scene, bringing diverse sounds and strong connections to events like Blast Music Fest.

name: DJ Joy
bio: DJ Joy is Joy Sgobba, a Seattle-based DJ originally from San Diego with an open-format, multi-genre approach built around pure dance-floor service. Her own profile names house, bass, breaks, disco, electronica, indie, hip-hop, old-school, funk, downtempo, Top 40, and more, which makes her less a genre specialist than a party translator. The through-line is simple: she loves music, reads rooms, and wants people moving whether the setting is a club, private party, festival, or community event. DJ Joy brings exactly what the name promises,range, momentum, and no shame in rocking the floor.

name: DJ Recess
bio: One of Seattle's most respected underground DJs, Recess has been in a long-term love affair with Seattle's underground music scene for nearly two decades. In 2003 he co-founded Shameless Productions, responsible for numerous legendary club nights. Nominated one of Seattle Weekly's best local DJs in 2006, his sets span house, disco, techno, electro, and breaks.

name: DJ SAID
bio: Hailing from Lagos, Nigeria, Said grew up in the very same community that produced the legendary Fela Kuti, father of afro-beat and innovator of funk. From a young age he was passionate about music, making mix tapes and DJing for his friends, and spend..For over a decade now, DJ Said of Fatsouls Records has successfully shown his dedication to music through his extraordinary events, music production and DJ skills. He has brought soulful East Coast-meets-Africa brand of percussion-based deep house to a distinct slice of the underground. Said has spun at his Atmosfere events and many other clubs around the world alongside the likes of Francois K, Joe Claussell, Danny Krivit, Tony Allen & Afrobeat 2000, Ron Trent, Kerri Chandler, Ron Trent, King Britt, Kenny Carpenter, Dennis Ferrer, Jerome Sydenham, Miguel Migs and countless others. His main influence is derived from Afrobeat, Highlife Jazz, Juju, and other music from his Yoruba root and numerous global music which he absorbed during his formative years. As a teenager, Said sorted after his sibling’s extraordinary musical interests and record collection. During the period, Said was quite connected to his neighbors, Fela Kuti's family organization. In 1984, Said moved from his hometown Lagos, Nigeria to England as a scholar. While in England, he gained a vast knowledge of the London club scene through direct experience and connections. When Said moved to the US in 1988, he became extremely close to the work of some the legendary artist whose music he played during his formative years. While getting an academic business education, Said maintained a strong presence in the San Francisco and New-York's underground club scene. By 1995, Said began residencies and guest appearances at clubs and private parties in the San Francisco Bay Area, New York and beyond. In 1999, Said launched Fatsouls Productions, a super sonic campaign outlet. Fatsouls' monthly “Atmosfere” event gained a vast and remarkable following in the San Francisco Bay Area and beyond during the years of 1999 – 2006. Fatsouls’ presentation of an extraordinary rotation of world class artist created a new nightlife scene in San Francisco that others have since attempted to emulate. Fatsouls events have been featured in URB Magazine, Straight No Chaser, San Francisco Bay Guardian, as well as in various local and international publications. Said currently heads Fatsouls Records, a San Francisco-based independent record label founded in 2007. Said still promotes various events with emphasis on House, Afrobeat and other Soulful Afro - Rhythms. He has a score of releases on his Fatsouls Records label and other top indie house music labels like Wave Music, Ibadan Records, and Chez Music among others. Keep your eyes and ears open.

name: DJ Three
bio: Miami-based DJ Christopher Milo, underground electronic music stalwart since the early 1990s. Has held residencies at NYC's Twilo, Cielo, Output, and more, plus global festival appearances.

name: DJ Weak Acid
bio: DJ Weak Acid, Nick Bigelow, is a Seattle DJ and electronic artist known for live modular techno. A Patchwerks-connected performer, Weak Acid has played Basement State shows alongside artists like Project 32 and Magic 3ye, and has appeared with the Patchwerks family at Monkey Loft. The sound is hands-on, analog, and built from machines in real time.

name: DNcrystal
bio: Seattle-based DJ, active in the Seattle underground electronic music scene. Played Reverie Society events at Monkey Loft alongside DJ Weak Acid and other Seattle DJs.

name: Dane Garfield
bio: Dane Garfield is a Seattle house DJ tied into the Flammable family, the long-running Sunday institution that has treated house music like church for more than three decades. Recent lineups place him at Chop Suey, Monkey Loft, Gas Works Park, and Shameless/Flammable collaborations, which says plenty about where he sits in the local ecosystem. His lane is not about celebrity gloss; it is about showing up for the room, the weekly, and the dancers who keep Seattle house culture alive. Dane brings working-DJ value: reliable heat, local continuity, and no need to over-explain the assignment.

name: Dave Pezzner
bio: Seattle-based DJ and producer born in Tarzana, CA. Released music on Dirtybird, Get Physical, Crosstown Rebels, Visionquest, and Strictly Rhythm. In-house composer at Can Can Cabaret.

name: David Scuba
bio: Los Angeles-based DJ and co-owner of the Superfreq brand with Mr.C. Known for dark and underground electronic music with decades of experience.

name: Derek Pavone
bio: Seattle DJ who has graced the Seattle music scene with talent and knowledge since the early 1990s. Active in Flammable and the Seattle underground scene.

name: Derrick Deep
bio: Seattle-based musician and DJ. Part of Deep Light Recordings, Uniting Souls Music, and the Elevated Frequencies collective. Active in the Seattle underground electronic music community.

name: Doza
bio: Doza is a Seattle open-format DJ who debuted under the name in 2017 and built his lane through community gatherings, club sets, and high-energy local rooms. His strength is range: reading the floor, moving quickly, and making genre shifts feel like part of the same conversation instead of a playlist panic. That kind of flexibility matters in a city where parties pull from house, hip-hop, bass, pop, and underground dance culture at once. Doza brings utility with personality,fast hands, crowd sense, and no fear of turning the room up.

name: Duncfoo
bio: Duncan Shea, known as Duncfoo, is a Seattle-based DJ and tech veteran. In 2004 he served as Technical DJ Expert and helped launch Serato's Scratch Live program, a pioneering contribution to modern DJing technology. He regularly headlines events like DJs in a Dive Bar at Blue Moon Seattle and participates in the Seattle Sunday stream series.

name: Dusty Booty Ranch with Sean Wood
EOC
bio: Seattle DJ known for house music. Active in Seattle nightlife alongside Miss Min D and other Seattle DJs.

name: Emily Song
bio: Emily Song is a seasoned Seattle DJ whose path started in 1997 with vinyl sets at underground warehouse parties across the Pacific Northwest. Now connected to Viva Recordings, she carries the history of Seattle house culture with the ease of someone who has lived through more than one version of the scene. Her sound is rooted in house music's social function: steady rhythm, warm selections, and the belief that a dance floor is built through trust rather than tricks. Emily Song brings elder-floor energy,deeply local, quietly powerful, and fully fluent in the language of late nights.

name: Evan Casey
bio: Los Angeles-based DJ and Desert Hearts resident. Known for dark, sinister, and deep electronic music, playing the latest hours at clubs and festivals.

name: Fern
bio: Fern is a Seattle-based DJ in the PNW electronic music community, active in underground dance events across the Pacific Northwest. A dedicated contributor to Seattle's underground scene, Fern brings soulful and underground electronic selections to dance floors, supporting the local community through performances at Shameless events and local venues.

name: Foxy McLeod
bio: House DJ based in Seattle, WA. Born in CA, raised in NC. Made her Shameless debut in 2021. Known for bringing energetic, community-centered sets to Seattle's underground.

name: Frida K
bio: Frida K is a Seattle-based house DJ and producer originally from Mexico. Her sound leans groovy, bright, and direct, with live sets and releases built for dancers who want motion without over-polish.

name: Fungineers
bio: Seattle musical collective described as "super group of musical magical weirdoes." Eclectic performers known for mixing styles and pushing the boundaries of fun.

name: Garth
bio: DJ Garth is a pioneering House music DJ who is largely credited with both defining and capturing the sound of San Francisco house music in the mid-1990s. As a recording artist, his production is featured on several San Francisco club anthems such as "T..Before a recent move to Los Angeles where he has been immersed in film acting, Garth spent two decades in San Francisco fanning the flames of conscious hell raising that existed there in generations past. Parties with his stamp include a coveted ten year residency at Come Unity, twenty three years with the legendary Wicked Sound System and more recently Back2Back, with right hand man Jenö. His sound is a radical fusion of Acid House, Space Disco & Psyche Rock. His solo All Night Long Loft parties in San Francisco continue to inspire dancers and critics alike. The Wicked crew reformed in 2011 for a thirteen city Twenty Years of Disco Glory Tour of the States & Japan. Garth spent seven weeks of 2010/2011 touring extensively with DJ Harvey in Australia and Japan. Bringing UK sound system culture to San Francisco in 1991, Wicked’s lawless full moon beach parties ushered in an era of Acid House hysteria that ripped through the west coast and spread into the heartland. Wicked are credited widely with kick-starting the City’s Acid House scene in the early 90’s, cementing its reputation as the red headed step child of Disco. Scoring a Tony Andrews designed Turbosound rig, they toured in a ’47 Greyhound bus whose previous owners were a gospel choir hitting the road to spread their own gospel across the States. Wicked was the pioneering sound system at Burning Man in 1995. Wicked Presents brought the cream of House luminaries to San Francisco including Tony Humphries, DJ Harvey, Doc Martin, Louis Vega, Roger S, Robert Owens, DJ Pierre, K Alexi, Chez Damier, Stacey Pullen, Alton M, Agent X, Kenny Hawkes, Three, Joe Claussel & Francois K. Garth holds court at many of the world’s best nightclubs including Eleven, Mago, Gold, Yellow, Townsend, 1015, The Roxy, Twilo, Fabric, The End, Horsemeat Disco, Sublevel, Thirtynine Hotel, Rex, Room, Lux & Zouk. He has been voted amongst America’s top DJs in Urb, XLR8R and DJ Magazines. For many years he pioneered all night Dub sets on the only renegade system permitted at Reggae on the River where Jamaican legends Mikey Dread and Junior Reid stopped by the bus to rock the mic. The same bus provided inspiration for a record label. Since ’98 Grayhound has released 50+ singles documenting an edgier West Coast sound. Artists include Wicked (Garth, Markie, Jenö & Thomas), Rocket, Perry Farrell, ETI, DJ Harvey, The Glimmers & Ray Mang, Darryl Pandy, Michoacan, DJ Rasoul, Nectar, Stranger, Steve Kotey & EBE. Many recording artists launched their careers on the label. His breakout anthem with E.T.I. ‘Twenty Minutes of Disco Glory’ shook things up internationally, praised by Muzik Magazine as one of the best dance singles of ’96. Since then he has produced 60 records under various pseudonyms working with Anthony Mansfield, Eric James (Rocket), Rob Doten (Crosstown Traffic) and Markie Mark or Jenö (Wicked). His remixes include Freeland, The Units, Faithless, Halo Varga, The Glimmers, Rob Rives, Dada Munchamonkey, Perry Farrell (Janes Addiction), Yabby You, Mutabaruka and Tribe Called Quest. He has compiled and mixed albums for Om, NRK, Thrive & Grayhound, Sex + Design Magazine & Farmers Daughter Hotel. Grayhound productions have been licensed to countless DJ mix CDs and Garth’s music can be found in two films – SF rave flick ‘Groove’ and Gus Van Sant’s ‘Speedway Junky’. Since 2007 he and collector/dealer James Glass have been waving the flag for Golden Goose re-edits, raiding their record collections & respectfully placing Disco/Rock obscurities under the knife as King & Hound. Remaining true to the original art form, he can be found playing all-vinyl sets out in Hawaii, Japan, Fiji, Korea, Spain, England, Greece, Portugal, France, Belgium, Germany, Mexico, Canada, Australia, Singapore, Thailand, even Indonesia and Malaysia, turning parties out upon request.

name: Gene Hunt
bio: Gene Hunt is truly one of Chicago’s Pioneers in the house music industry since the early 80s. He has been one of the top jocks moving and educating crowds and music lovers for the past 25 years of his career. Playing at the hottest venues around the world such as The Music Box, Medusas, Green Dolphin, Smart Bar, Metro, the Mid, XOXO – UK, Hectors Lounge – Scotland, Dimensions Festival – Croatia, Field Maneuvers – Oxford UK, 212 – Leeds and many more. This legend has worked with some of the heaviest hitters in the game, such as, Ron Hardy, Frankie Knuckles, Farley Jack Master Funk, and Kenny Dope of M.A.W. Gene’s first EP in 1987; Living in the land was released on an old classic label where Chicago house music was exposed called Traxx. He then moved onto several UK labels such as, Djax, Hybrid, Mc Projects, Step 2 House, Svek and Rush hour where he made is mark abroad. Mr. Hunt has released his albums “In Sound” on Little Angel Recording – Harley & Muscle, and “Seasoned” on Unified Records. He’s recently joined the Aria team for Roland, sat on a panel and was interviewed for his opinion on their Pro-Audio Studio Gear for Music Production products. Gene has appeared as headliner at many festivals such as; Outside Lands, Glastonbury, Firefly, and Coachella in the Heineken Dome and performed at Chicago’s own Summer Dance, down town. Nominated in 2016 and 2 times back to back winner of the NBC Chicago Music Award, Gene was listed as number one in 2011 for top dance album in the United Kingdom, with his “Dance Track Volume One” album. He’s performed at Boiler Room London and Boiler Room Chicago. He won the Global Mix Award, and performed at the Congress Theater who then, was interviewed for the release of the History of House Music Chicago LP CD and DVD. Gene also has a custom line of headphones designed by his very self for some of the top house & hip hop legends in the industry; he was also the apprentice of the late legendary Ron Hardy US Studio, so keep a lookout!

name: GeoD
bio: Long-time participant in the San Francisco underground scene, now residing in Seattle. Plays deep, progressive, tech, and melodic house and techno.

name: Gina Turner
bio: New York-born internationally touring DJ, producer, radio host, yoga teacher, and label boss. Voted Best NYC DJ by Village Voice. Releases on Ultra Music, Ministry of Sound, Defected, and more.

name: Ginkgo
bio: Ginkgo is Robert Ginkgo, a Portland-linked DJ and co-curator of Believe You Me PDX and Your Sunday Best. His RA history spans Portland and Seattle, while his SoundCloud points to residency work with Believe You Me, Silicon Radio, and Process PDX. His lane is broader Pacific Northwest house culture with a Portland center of gravity: warm, underground, crate-conscious, and built for rooms that like subtle pressure.

name: GriffinGrrl
bio: GriffinGrrl is a Seattle DJ and dance-music catalyst with roots in the 1990s Los Angeles rave scene. That history gives her sets a lived-in understanding of rave culture as more than aesthetics: rhythm, release, community, and a willingness to chase the ecstatic edge. Her C89.5 guest-mix presence points to the same thing her local reputation does,an energetic selector who knows how to bring momentum without losing personality. GriffinGrrl brings veteran spark with West Coast muscle and a direct line to why people fell in love with this music in the first place.

name: HAÜS/GASM
bio: HAÜS\\GASM is a queer Seattle DJ project built for house, tech, techno, indie dance, and disco pressure. The name says party, but the lane is sharper than novelty: sweaty, playful club music with enough range to work a Breakfast Club dance floor and enough attitude to keep it from going beige. Their sets move through queer nightlife's best instincts,release, flirtation, rhythm, and a little chaos in the corners. HAÜS\\GASM brings exactly the right temperature: hot, unruly, and aimed at bodies first.

name: Hanssen
bio: Seattle electronic music producer and DJ (also known as Jacob London). Co-host of Expansions on KEXP radio, blending electronic and dance genres.

name: Hector J Rodriguez
bio: Seattle-based DJ, producer, and label founder. Active in the Seattle music community, bringing fire to the dance floor at venues throughout the city.

name: HollyLouYah
bio: Seattle DJ and co-host of the Check the Volume weekly Twitch livestream party. Known for indie dance and house music. A longtime fixture in the Seattle electronic music scene with Shameless.

name: Hyasynth
bio: Seattle DJ Karen Baskett with deep, soulful, disco-influenced sound. Moved from NYC to Seattle in 2004. Flammable Sundays resident DJ at Chop Suey.

name: I.S.H
bio: Seattle DJ and music producer Ismael Lukata, with Palestinian heritage and Spanish background. Making waves in Seattle's scene with bold, danceable sounds.

name: J.Phlip
bio: J.Phlip is Jessica Phillippe, a San Francisco-based DJ and producer who became one of Dirtybird's founding originals and helped carry its bass-heavy, playful house sound around the world. Her résumé includes serious rooms and festivals,Movement Detroit, Coachella, Fabric London, Space Ibiza,but the real point is her instinct for weird, tough, body-jacking records. She can go funky, dark, stripped, or rude without losing the floor's trust. J.Phlip brings West Coast club mischief at expert level: dirty low end, sharp turns, and a selector's refusal to behave.

name: James Flavour
bio: James Flavour is Felix Eder, the German DJ and producer best known as one half of Dirt Crew alongside Break 3000. Since the mid-2000s, Dirt Crew's mix of old-school house and new-school techno has earned international dance-floor traction, with releases and remixes tied to labels such as MBF, Moodmusic, Highgrade, Trapez, and their own Dirt Crew Recordings. Flavour's sound carries that label-head sensibility: groovy, sturdy, and built from records that know their way around a sweaty room. James Flavour brings classic European house-techno muscle with enough funk to keep the edges loose.

name: Jamie Schwabl
bio: Jamie Schwabl is a Seattle DJ and producer connected to the city's underground through Shameless, Tremulant, and related local circles. Her releases and sets sit in the functional zone where house, techno, and after-hours mood can overlap without announcing every transition. She brings the kind of scene-level presence that matters more than hype: showing up, developing sound, and helping keep the local current moving. Jamie brings focused hometown pressure,clean, grounded, and built for dancers who stay late.

name: Jaogaz
bio: Argentinian house and techno DJ and producer (Juan Ogaz). Has played alongside Tim Green, Lee Burridge, and others. Active in the Seattle and NYC underground scenes.

name: Jared Love
bio: Jared Love is a Vancouver, BC-based DJ and producer whose music builds on influences ranging from jazz and R\&B to early electronica. Emerging in the early 2010s in Vancouver's burgeoning underground, he earned residencies at staples like Gorg-O-Mish Afterhours and Soma Music Hub. His releases have charted internationally, including collaborations with Jay Tripwire, and he has performed at notable events including opening for Traumer. His hypnotic, groove-driven style is rooted in Vancouver's underground dance floor culture.

name: Jason Peters
bio: San Francisco DJ, producer, and label boss at Roam Recordings.Jason Peters is a DJ, producer and head of Roam Recordings, a San Francisco imprint defining a distinct corner of underground dance music. For over 25 years, he has run the label and released music, developing a sound rooted in psychedelic, cosmic, Italo and acid-tinged club music. His forward-looking productions, including “Satellites” and “In the Dark,” have reached the upper tiers of Beatport’s charts, while his catalog reflects old-school rave roots with releases on Bay Area institution Wicked Records and UK imprint Hooj Choons. His DJ sets reflect that same arc—blending cosmic disco and hypnotic, psychedelic textures with an intuitive approach shaped by experience—meeting the crowd where they are and guiding them through a cohesive journey. A long-standing presence in the Bay Area, Peters is a regular at the city’s more discerning institutions, including Sunset Sound System, Public Works, Monarch and The Great Northern. He has also recently teamed up with Sirens LA to co-promote a new event series in San Francisco, extending his role in cultivating a community around this sound. Internationally, he has appeared at SundaySunday, CRSSD Festival, Block 9 at Glastonbury, Red Light Radio, Kater Blau and Alfresco Festival.

name: Jason Woo
bio: Jason Woo is a Seattle DJ connected to the Studio 4/4 and Monkey Loft circuit, with listings placing him on support bills for Lost Desert, Eli Escobar, Sama' Abdulhadi, Opulent Temple's Sacred Dance, and Shameless' Memorial Day Hijinks. He has appeared alongside Seattle names including Copeland, Ramiro, Zeebo, Brian Lyons, Sean Majors, Fouad Masoud, and Simone BG. His sets bring a polished house and techno sensibility with dependable local support-slot energy.

name: Jen Woolfe
bio: Jen Woolfe is a Shameless resident with a long arc through Seattle, San Francisco, and back again, carrying deep house and techno with the perspective of someone who has lived in multiple West Coast dance ecosystems. Her sets lean into groove, mood, and the kind of resident-level patience that makes a party feel held instead of merely programmed. She understands Shameless from the inside, which means the selections can serve the room without flattening her personality. Jen brings a trusted hand to the booth: deep, warm, and quietly built for the long haul.
aliases: Jen Woolfe (Loft)|Jen Woolfe (Mezzanine - Shameless 22 Year Anniversary)

name: Jesse Leer
bio: Seattle native DJ and producer. On Uniting Souls Music, Hood Politics Records, OFDM Records, and Feral One Records. Known for eclectic house, techno, and breaks.

name: Joe Bellingham
bio: Joe Bellingham is a Seattle DJ, producer, and live electronic act with a long Shameless history, joining the collective in 2009 and performing at Electric Tea Garden and other local rooms. His live-facing work points to a producer and performer rooted in Seattle underground electronic music rather than a straight DJ-only profile.

name: Joe Pemberton
bio: Seattle DJ featured in Shameless Audiocast. Active in the Seattle underground electronic music community.

name: Joey Webb
bio: San Francisco native now residing in the Seattle area. DJ with 20+ years of dance floor experience across NYC, Houston, SF, and Seattle. Known for cross-genre sets.

name: Jon Lee
bio: Jon Lee is a Seattle DJ with roots stretching back to 1994 and a long-running commitment to deep, minimal, and underground house. As the founder of Concepts Records, he has been part of the city's connective tissue between local selectors, producers, and dancers who prefer substance over flash. His releases and mixes keep a steady focus on groove, restraint, and late-night utility,the kind of sound that rewards people who stay on the floor past the obvious moment. Jon Lee brings veteran pressure with a clean hand: patient, deep, and built for rooms that know how to listen while they move.

name: Jon Lemmon
bio: Jon Lemmon is a Seattle DJ, producer, radio host, and founder of Viva Recordings, one of the city's long-running deep-house institutions. His work around Viva connects label culture, DJ craft, and community programming, keeping Seattle's house pulse steady across clubs, radio, and released music. Behind the decks, Lemmon favors deep, warm, functional grooves that build trust before they build drama. He brings label-head discipline with Northwest looseness: soulful pressure, clean momentum, and a room-first sense of what house music is for.

name: Josh Verse
bio: Seattle DJ inspired by KCMU's DJ Riz. Active in the Shameless and Seattle underground electronic music community.

name: Julie Herrera
bio: A California native raised in Seattle from age 13, Julie Herrera has been a pillar of Seattle's underground dance culture since taking to the decks in 1996. Her long residency at Re-bar's Flammable night, recognized as the longest-running underground house night in the world at 29+ years and now at Chop Suey, is the stuff of local legend. Her journey spans classic to modern house, techno, tech-house, disco, and afrobeat.

name: Kadeejah Streets
bio: Eddie Cuscavage, aka Kadeejah Streets, has been actively contributing to Seattle's underground community for nearly two decades. He is head of the Dirty Ice Crew, which produces events from roller rinks to music festivals, and helps produce DJ Sharlese's monthly radio slot on KEXP's Expansions show. A cult figure in Seattle's underground.

name: Kadeem Taves
bio: Kadeem Rashad, known simply as Kadeem, is a Tacoma-based DJ and student of the game who plays house, techno, and ghettotech. He regularly performs with Viva Recordings and Shameless at Monkey Loft's Reverie Society Sunday day parties, sharing the bill with Rick Preston, Mr. Linden, and Hector J Rodriguez.

name: Kevin Knapp
bio: World-touring DJ and co-owner of Plump Records. Known for releases on Hot Creations, Crosstown Rebels, Dirtybird, and more. Regular performer at major festivals.

name: Kid Hops
bio: Seattle DJ and KEXP radio host presenting reggae on Positive Vibrations and electronic music on Expansions. Releases on Hospital Records, Ninja Tune, Get Physical.

name: Kinda
bio: KINDA is a Seattle DJ tied to the Studio 4/4 resident crew, with public mixes on C89.5 and Peculiar Beats. Their SoundCloud also identifies KINDA as one half of Ravennas and one half of Pinky Promise, giving the project a clear local context. The sound points toward Seattle club culture with a taste for house and electronic sets that can work both radio and room.

name: Kinjo
bio: Macedonia-native and longtime Seattle resident. DJ, producer, promoter, and record label head active in the Pacific Northwest music community.

name: Konifer
bio: Seattle DJ luminary and longtime fixture in the PNW electronic music scene. Active in Innerflight Music and related Seattle underground electronic events.

name: Kyle Douglas
bio: Kyle Douglas is a Chicago-born, Seattle-based DJ and selector with a deep-house center and a mentor's relationship to the local scene. His House Cleaning Records link points to the practical work of keeping house music circulating: sharing records, shaping taste, and helping the next wave understand the culture behind the groove. His sets lean into warmth, swing, and patience rather than cheap impact. Kyle brings caretaker energy with dance-floor purpose,deep crates, clean intent, and a steady hand on the room.

name: LGSP
bio: Seattle DJ and Uniting Souls Music resident. Her exuberance for art is directly tied to her passion for music, playing deep house and electronic music.

name: Lauren Ritter
bio: Lauren Ritter is a New York-based DJ, producer, and founder of Rift Vision, known for house music that favors groove, patience, and emotional detail over easy peak-time tricks. A longtime presence around the Resolute orbit, she brings the kind of selector discipline that turns a room slowly, letting rhythm and atmosphere do the heavy lifting. Her productions and DJ sets sit in that sweet spot between deep, trippy, and functional,music for dancers who like their hypnosis with a little bite. Lauren is the after-hours compass: elegant pressure, strange corners, and a pulse that keeps unfolding.

name: Leah York
bio: Leah York is a Seattle DJ and producer working across Latin club, techno, groove, reggaeton, perreo, dembow, future house, and Latin house energy. Her public links point to original releases like "Menealo," video sets, and a lane built around movement-first, bilingual, global dance sounds. She is not just playing generic club records; she is pulling Seattle into a sharper Latin electronic conversation. Leah brings color, bounce, and the kind of rhythm that opens up the floor fast.

name: Lee Reynolds
bio: A spirited English DJ who crossed the Atlantic to Los Angeles, Lee Reynolds found his calling in electronic music after a BMX career. He refined his craft at West Coast institutions like Moontribe and Lightning in a Bottle, and in 2011 co-founded Desert Hearts alongside Mikey Lion and Marbs. Known as Papa Lee, he brings a spiritual dimension to house, techno, acid, and breaks.

name: Levi Clark
bio: Levi Clark is a longtime Shameless resident whose musical background runs wider than a single lane, pulling from rock, electronica, hip-hop, classical, blues, jazz, breaks, and techno. Early event listings already had him sharing stages with national headliners while sharpening a taste for dirty, body-moving electronic music. These days, his presence on Shameless lineups reads less like a booking note and more like infrastructure: someone who understands the room, the crew, and the culture from the inside. Levi brings the kind of resident energy every real party needs,flexible, grounded, and fully committed to making people dance.

name: Lola K
bio: Lola K is a Seattle-based DJ and producer pushing bright, club-ready house with enough grit to keep it underground. Her releases and credits connect her to Late Night Munchies, Brainjack, and Paradise Music, placing her in a lane where local hustle meets proper dance-floor utility. Behind the decks she keeps things agile, moving through house, tech-house, and late-night rollers without losing the party thread. Lola K brings hometown voltage: polished enough to travel, raw enough to keep the room sweating.

name: Lubelski
bio: Lubelski is a Los Angeles-based producer and DJ whose hardware-heavy approach turns house music into something loose, psychedelic, and joyfully unpredictable. His catalog spans Dirtybird, Desert Hearts, Crosstown Rebels, Gruuv, Club Bad, and MOAN, with the 2022 album Happy Accidents showing the full range of his studio instincts. As a DJ, he has carried that weird-funk sensibility into major clubs and festivals including Lightning in a Bottle, Ultra Miami, Holy Ship\! Wrecked, Splash House, Elsewhere, Space Miami, and Public Works. Lubelski is a natural fit: playful machine music with serious low-end craft and no interest in coloring inside the lines.

name: Lucky Cheese
bio: Lucky Cheese is a Seattle DJ with roots in Chile and Washington. Her sets move through eclectic club sounds with a warm, playful touch, bringing local underground energy without getting locked into one lane.

name: Luke Mandala
bio: Luke Mandala is a San Diego-based live electronic performer who bridges acoustic musicianship and machine-driven dance music. His setup folds in live wind instruments, drumming, and electronic production, giving the performance a human pulse that can stretch beyond standard DJ architecture. The result lands somewhere between ritual, jam, and club system: organic textures wrapped around dance-floor motion. Luke Mandala brings livewire festival energy with actual musicianship behind it,earthy, psychedelic, and built to move.

name: Lumi
bio: Finnish DJ and producer centered around deep, cerebral, and introspective house and techno. Dark, ethereal visuals reminiscent of her Finnish heritage. Regular at Seattle's Monkey Loft.

name: Malibu Delica
bio: Seattle DJ and enthusiastic selector with a crate spanning 90s rave to current sounds. Regular at Monkey Loft.

name: Mango & Ginger
bio: Brazilian DJ selecta duo KA and MATA, bringing global sounds and deep grooves to the dancefloor.

name: Marbs
bio: Matt "Marbs" Marabella is a Los Angeles-based DJ and producer, co-founder of Desert Hearts, and head of Desert Hearts Black. Influenced by Pink Floyd, Tool, and underground hip-hop, his sets command the dancefloor with sounds of mystical darkness and whimsical tribal rhythms. He founded Desert Hearts Black in 2019 to explore darker, more hypnotic sounds.

name: Meow Pals
bio: Meow Pals is a Seattle/Tremulant-linked DJ project documented on Kremwerk and DICE listings from the city's deeper club ecosystem. The name appears on Tremulant bills alongside Niki Sadeki, Zeebo, Parham, Captain Tremulant, Monotropa, and Jon-Paul Petry, placing the project in a psychedelic, melodic, small-room lane. The feel is niche and local in the best way: real scene presence, weird corners, and enough mystery to keep the room curious.

name: Michael Manahan
bio: Michael Manahan has been a mover in Pacific Northwest underground arts and music culture for more than two decades. Based between the Cascade foothills, Seattle, and Portland, he works as a DJ, event producer, music producer, and creative force behind Hunt & Gather Recordings and Cascadia NW Arts and Music Festival. His sets sit comfortably between ambient depth, bass weight, and deep club focus, which makes him more than just another name on a lineup. Manahan brings regional history, outdoor-festival spirit, and the kind of patient pressure that knows how to stretch a night open.

name: Mikey Lion
bio: Part mythical desert harlequin, part modern soul conductor, Mikey Lion is more than a DJ. A San Diego native, he co-founded Desert Hearts Records and the Desert Hearts Festival movement, which started as a gathering of 200 in the Mojave in 2012 and grew into one of the most celebrated boutique festivals in North America.

name: Milk & Honey
bio: Milk & Honey is the Seattle duo of Everett Leftside and Zeebo, documented around Kremwerk's Tremulant orbit and centered on warm, drum-forward house music. Everett Leftside is also known as a MIND at LARGE resident, with sets moving through afro, melodic, deep, and tech tracks built around rhythm and introspection. Zeebo anchors the other half of the pairing, tying the project back to Seattle's deeper house community.

name: Milo Hous
bio: Seattle-based EDM music events collective and DJ crew representing the Pacific Northwest underground scene. Milo House Presents hosts multi-stage events at venues like Vue Seattle and Q Nightclub, spotlighting local and emerging artists in house, dubstep, and electronic music.

name: Miss Min.D
bio: Miss Min.D is Mindy Christman, a Seattle multi-genre DJ, producer, promoter, and community builder whose roots run from Wyoming into the city's underground dance scene. Active in Seattle since 2004 and DJing since the vinyl era, she helped found Skunky Beats, DJ4NORML, and Fraktured, then became one of the city's most trusted names for breaks, house, bass, and drum & bass pressure. Public bios and interviews point to long-running residencies and performances across Last Supper Club, Club Contour, Noc Noc, The High Dive, Still Liquor, Kremwerk, and C89.5. Miss Min.D brings a rare mix of floor craft and community muscle: high-energy selections, emotional range, and a resident-level ability to make a room move without flattening her personality.

name: Miss Shelrawka
bio: Shelrawka, also billed as Miss Shelrawka, is a Seattle DJ who made her mark locally after relocating from New York City in 2008. Recent Kremwerk listings describe her as a highly sought-after selector with more than 20 years of experience, precise mixing, and a multi-genre approach shaped by techno, minimal, and underground club music.

name: Moist Towelette
bio:
For well over 20 years, Luying (also known as DJ Moist Towellette) has been a cornerstone of our local underground music scene. As a dedicated member of the Shameless crew and long time friend of the Chaos BurningMan crew, she has worked tirelessly behind the scenes to ensure that every event at venues like Rebar, Kremwerk, Pony, and Massive Club sounded great! Whether it’s a live act, DJ set, or comedy routine, Luying’s expertise in sound tech and production has made countless events successful.

Luying is not just a hardworking professional; she is a champion for the marginalized, always working with grace, dignity, and love. Her contributions to our community are immeasurable

name: Monotropa
bio: Monotropa is a Seattle-area DJ connected to the deeper, feel-forward side of the local underground. RA listings place them with Diffusion and CARAVAN lineups, moving through deep house, global sounds, and intimate basement energy. The existing bio had the right idea, but the story is bigger than "sensual" selections: Monotropa fits a Seattle lineage of patient, human-scale dance floors. In Shameless terms, expect warmth, slow-burn tension, and a room that gets pulled in rather than pushed around.

name: Mr. Linden
bio: Jeremy Linden is an Alaska-raised, Seattle-based DJ, designer, and producer who is an integral part of the local underground electronic music community. He is Creative Director at Underscore Recordings, one half of the DJ duo Lo|Key with Hector J. Rodriguez, and a resident at Train Car House Party, Soft Option, and Back of the House.

name: Mrakulous
bio: Seattle-based DJ active in the local electronic music community.

name: Nancy Dru
bio: Nancy Dru is a Vancouver, BC-based techno DJ and producer with more than 20 years in electronic music. Her path started with online radio in the 1990s, moved through IDM, jungle, and ambient production, and evolved into dense, danceable techno marked by syncopated beats and acid bite. She has played alongside artists including Steffi, Carl Craig, and Rødhåd, with festival appearances tied to Mutek, Decibel, and Communikey. Nancy Dru brings Pacific Northwest techno with real architecture: brainy, bodily, and built from long-term commitment.

name: Nark
bio: Nark is Kevin Kauer, a Seattle/Palm Springs DJ, producer, photographer, designer, and nightlife builder behind Nark Magazine, Bottom Forty, and Massive. His work helped push Seattle queer club culture toward something stranger, sexier, and more open, with parties that made room for disco, acid house, techno, drag, fashion, and late-night weirdness. Behind the decks, Nark brings analog warmth, queer-club pressure, and the confidence of someone who helped build the room he is playing in.

name: Natural Magic
bio: Portland, OR DJ duo (Michael McKinnon and Matthew Quiet). Host the Limited Edition parties and I Believe It's Magic radio show.

name: Nayiram
bio: Nayiram is a Ghanaian-born, Seattle-based DJ pushing Afrobeats, Amapiano, Afro House, dancehall, and global club sounds through the city's nightlife. His work around The Afrobeats Party and Global Bounce has helped give Seattle a consistent, community-centered space for African music and Black dance-floor culture. On the decks, he moves like a connector, folding popular anthems, underground edits, and diasporic rhythms into one room. That energy opens the floor wider and makes the party feel bigger than one scene.

name: Nazo
bio: Nazo is a Seattle-based house DJ originally from Soweto, South Africa, bringing Afro House and African-influenced electronic music into the local underground. His sound sits where rhythm, heritage, and club function overlap, favoring percussion, warmth, and a grounded sense of movement over empty spectacle. In a city often pulled toward European and American dance lineages, Nazo adds a needed rhythmic vocabulary and a different emotional center. He brings deep diasporic groove: sun, sweat, and a floor that moves from the hips outward.

name: Nick Bertossi
bio: A highly sought-after Seattle-based DJ and music producer known for explosive, tribal-infused sets that fuse raw energy with technical mastery. Originally from Canada, Nick has spent over 17 years building his name in the Seattle underground while playing internationally.

name: Nick Carroll
bio: Nick Carroll is a Seattle DJ, booker, and co-founder of secondnature. Through secondnature, Research, and Kremwerk-connected work, he helped carve out space for sharper techno, house, bass pressure, and late-night programming in the Pacific Northwest. As a DJ, he can move from heavy techno into house, classic dubstep, and disco without making it feel like a resume exercise. Nick brings range, taste, and a little danger to the floor.

name: Nina Sol
bio: Nina Sol is an Oakland-based DJ with a deep, soulful touch and a collector's ear for music that makes people feel held while they move. Her sets draw from soul, funk, house, disco, and diasporic groove, with residencies and Bay Area parties built around community as much as selection. She is not a rush-the-room kind of DJ; she lets warmth do the work until the floor is fully open. Nina brings sunlight, rhythm, and that rare kind of dance-floor generosity.

name: Noise Complaint
bio: Noise Complaint is a Seattle DJ and events collective built around thoughtfully curated house and techno nights. With long ties to the Kremwerk/Timbre Room ecosystem, the project has helped create space for touring guests, local selectors, and dance floors that want more intention than bottle-service wallpaper. The name is cheeky, but the work is serious: building rooms where underground music can feel social, sweaty, and specific. In the Shameless universe, Noise Complaint fits naturally,part promoter brain, part resident heat, and fully committed to keeping Seattle loud in the right ways.

name: Nordic Soul
bio: Nordic Soul is Sean Horton, the DJ, producer, composer, and Decibel Festival founder whose fingerprints are all over Seattle electronic music. Under the Nordic Soul name, he channels the same wide-angle taste that made Decibel matter: techno, house, hip-hop, soul, live instrumentation, and emotionally charged electronic music without genre tunnel vision. His work behind the scenes helped connect Seattle to global experimental and club culture, while his DJ sets keep that history physical and floor-focused. In the Shameless world, Nordic Soul is both elder statesman and active voltage,deep roots, deep crates, and a room-first sense of momentum.

name: OFF99
bio: Seattle-based DJ duo known for their sets at Deck'd Out and Shameless events. Part of the Seattle underground electronic music community.

name: Onyx Ocean
bio:
After just over a year of DJ experience in the PNW scene, Onyx Ocean quickly established herself as a force to be reckoned with.

Her most notable recent appearance was as the opening act for The Crystal Method.

Throughout 2025, Onyx shared lineups with A Hundred Drums, UFO!, Phantom45, Mikey Lion, Marbs, Brett Johnson, Creach, and local legends Riz'N Robs (Won Love), Tony H, Luke Mandala, Iris, and Raica.

In her first year performing, she played sets for Emergence DNB, Quantum, Deep N Bass, Be My Guest, The Petricorps Sound System, Train Car House Party, Elevated Frequencies, Shameless Productions, Innerflight Music, Uniting Souls, Imagine Music and Arts Festival, and Cascadia NW Arts and Music Festival.

With a history in the festival and dance scene dating back to the age of 7, Onyx eventually began working in event production. She developed a deep love for her local underground scene, and a profound respect for those who run it. She is currently the youngest core crew member on the Cascadia festival team.

At the age of 11, Onyx completed the FCC training and became the youngest certified DJ on the community radio station KAOS. She and her mom are a DJ duo known as "Shaman's Dose." Together, they host "Kitty Kat Cul-de-sac" every other Sunday.

Onyx prides herself on her track selection. During any given day, you may catch her spinning Acid, Breakbeats, Drum & Bass, UKG, House, Techno, and Psytrance.

Whether you find her at a renegade, warehouse, or festival stage, her sets are crafted with intentional energy and emotional depth. Prepare yourself for a journey through dynamic atmosphere and driving basslines.

name: Pelehon
bio: Seattle DJ who began DJing in 2019 and is a member of the Tremulant collective. Known for cosmic and cerebral electronic music.

name: Peter Evans
bio: Peter Evans is a Seattle-based Viva Recordings DJ with a steady archive of local sets, radio mixes, and club recordings. His public SoundCloud history includes Viva Recordings dates, C89.5 The Vortex, Proton Featured Artist, Diffusion, and multiple Monkey Loft appearances. His sound sits in the deep-house lane: polished, patient, and built around groove instead of spectacle.

name: Poof
bio: Benji Rubinoff, aka Poof, is a resident DJ at Massive Club and organizer of the queer underground event Squelch Seattle. Their sets blend disco, funk, and deep house with community spirit, creating inclusive dancefloor experiences that center queer joy and belonging.

name: Porky
bio: Porky is David Leon, the San Diego native and Desert Hearts wildcard whose lighthearted name hides a very serious commitment to the party. Raised around the same Southern California underground orbit as the Desert Hearts crew, he built his reputation through colorful, high-energy house and techno sets at Desert Hearts weekenders, Burning Man, Tulum, and City Hearts events across North America. His style is playful without being disposable: weird techno corners, house crowd-pleasers, big personality, and a selector's instinct for turning records into moments.

name: Project 32
bio: Project 32 is Steven, a Seattle electronic artist whose public profile keeps the description simple: synths and things. Listings place Project 32 in the city's live electronic circuit, including Kremwerk support for LA techno figure Truncate and Shameless' Memorial Day Hijinks at Monkey Loft. The project leans hardware-minded and direct, with compact live electronic energy built for the room.

name: Rachel Vick
bio: Seattle-based vocalist, songwriter, guitarist, pianist, and DJ. Born in Cleveland, OH and now thriving in Seattle. Known for both live performances and DJ sets.

name: Raica
bio: Raica is the ambient and experimental electronic project of Seattle artist Chloe Harris, a veteran DJ, label operator, and lifelong record obsessive. Harris started in record stores young, began DJing as a teenager, and went on to help run Further Records while developing Raica as a space for improvisational, textural electronics. The sound drifts between ambient, techno, and hardware-driven abstraction, built for deep listening but never disconnected from the body. Raica brings a different kind of intensity: fog, circuitry, pressure, and the kind of patience that makes a room lean in.

name: Ramiro
bio: Ramiro is Ramiro Gutierrez, founder of Uniting Souls and one of Seattle house music's true community architects. Since founding Uniting Souls in 1997 and making Seattle home in 2000, he has produced and performed at hundreds of events, helped build the Uniting Souls Music label, and earned descriptions like "Seattle house music institution" and "West Coast legend." His DJ sets are built around connection, storytelling, groove, and the belief that house music thrives when the room feels welcome. Ramiro brings the full package: history, heart, label-head discipline, and dance-floor generosity.

name: Rascal Rae
bio: Seattle electronic artist and DJ. Known for hardware-based live sets and a whimsical approach to music production.

name: Repoman
bio:
Repoman is a Seattle DJ, promoter, and graphic designer — and co-creator of the highly influential Night Moves party series. His sets pull from EBM, electro, wave, acid, synth, and darker club strains, built for dancers who like the room dim and the drums metallic.

Alongside fellow Seattle DJ Wax Witch (founder of Babe Night), Repoman co-runs Night Moves, a crew widely recognized for curating dark, high-energy, club-kid-friendly events including the celebrated Blood Rave and Acid Punx parties. He is a staple of the Seattle nightlife circuit, frequently spinning at Massive and Kremwerk and hosting b2b sets with prominent local DJs.

Beyond the decks, Repoman (also credited as @repomqn) doubles as an event photographer, capturing the raw energy of the Seattle underground scene.

name: Rex
bio: Rex is a Seattle DJ with an open-format style built around turntable skill, quick transitions, and crowd-focused wordplay. His sets pull from soulful rhythms, club energy, and dance-floor instincts, giving him a flexible lane in Seattle's underground party circuit.

name: Rick Preston
bio: Rick Preston is a Bay Area legend who helped lay the foundation of the West Coast underground dance scene. A Viva Recordings artist from day one, his career began in the early 80s immersed in disco and funk. His track "Spring Fling" was featured on Mark Farina's San Francisco Sessions Vol. 1, and he has been a fixture at Seattle's Monkey Loft for Viva and Shameless events.

name: Riz & Rob
bio: Seattle DJ duo featuring Riz Rollins and Rob Green. Riz is a 35+ year KEXP radio personality, venerated Seattle fixture at gay, hip-hop and electronic dance parties.

name: Riz Rollins
bio: Riz Rollins is a Seattle institution: DJ Riz, longtime KEXP voice, Expansions host, and a defining presence in the city's gay, Black, hip-hop, and electronic dance communities. His history runs from Chicago roots to decades of Seattle clubs, radio booths, basements, and dance floors, with sets that can move through soul, gospel, funk, hip-hop, house, drum & bass, and left-field magic without breaking the spell. Riz does not just play records; he tells people who they are allowed to become for a few hours. He brings blessing and mischief in equal measure: reverend, selector, troublemaker, and living archive.

name: Rob Noble
bio: Rob Noble is a Seattle drum & bass and bass-music DJ with deep ties to the DEEP N BASS world and the wider PNW underground. Event bios describe him as a dedicated performer with more than a decade behind the decks, known primarily for drum & bass while refusing to let genre labels fence in his taste. That breadth makes his sets hit differently: melodic, heavy, fluid, and willing to move between speed, pressure, and atmosphere. Rob Noble brings low-end discipline from a scene that knows endurance, precision, and how to make a system work.

name: Robag Wruhme
bio: Robag Wruhme is Gabor Schablitzki, the German producer and DJ whose catalog has become a benchmark for playful, detailed, emotionally strange house and techno. Known through Kompakt, Pampa, Musik Krause, and the Wighnomy Brothers lineage, he makes dance music that can be microscopic and goofy, elegant and crooked, all at once. His productions reward close listening, but they also understand that the club is not a lecture hall. Robag brings master-level weirdness,melody, mischief, and precision folded into one unmistakable bounce.

name: Rosie
bio: Rosie is a Seattle yoga instructor connected to Reverie Society's Shameless yoga sessions. Her role sits in the softer edge of the roster: movement, music, breath, and the daytime community side of the crew's programming.

name: Sappho
bio: Portland-based DJ, musical artist, event producer, and co-owner of Koritsi Komma Records. DJing since 1999, starting in Bellingham, WA before relocating to Portland. Known for eclectic, LGBTQ+-inclusive sets and advocacy. Resident at Bottom Forty (Seattle) and Underground SF; also plays CC Slaughter's and The Eagle in Portland.

name: Saqib
bio: Saqib is a Brooklyn-based DJ, producer, and label head whose records move with the loose, hypnotic confidence of a room that knows it is in good hands. Founder of Beats On Time, he has built a catalog around forward-leaning house, indie dance, and left-field club music, with releases and affiliations stretching through Kindisch, Desert Hearts, Abracadabra, and Acid Tourism. His sets carry that same signature: elastic percussion, warm psychedelia, and enough narrative lift to keep dancers locked in without sanding down the weird edges. Saqib lands as a true party architect,musical, mischievous, and tuned to the communal side of the dance floor.

name: Sassmouth
bio: Sassmouth is a Chicago-based DJ with Seattle ties whose sets move playfully through techno, house, acid, electro, and whatever else can make a room snap awake. A smartbar-level selector with a high-energy, eclectic reputation, she brings Midwest club knowledge without making it feel academic. Her style is fun in the dangerous sense: quick turns, weird records, and a refusal to let the night settle into autopilot. Sassmouth brings exactly the right kind of trouble,sharp, queer-adjacent club energy with a grin and a kick drum.

name: Sean Majors
bio: Seattle DJ, founder of Upper Left Events and Studio 44. Part of the LOST DJ crew and Monkey Loft resident. Known for bringing top-tier underground bookings to Seattle.

name: Shaner
bio: Shaner is a Seattle DJ tied to the modern Shameless Breakfast Club circle, with public listings placing him on the 2026 New Year's Day lineup at Monkey Loft and a SoundCloud profile for the SHANERISM series. His footprint is local, social, and built around the long-haul Breakfast Club format. Shaner brings lived-in Seattle energy, steady dance-floor instincts, and a sound that fits naturally beside Recess, Riz, WD4D, Sho-Nuph, Veta Vitali, and the rest of the crew.

name: Sharlese
bio: Sharlese is Sharlese Metcalf, a Seattle DJ, KEXP host, producer, and tireless local-music connector with roots in radio going back to 2001. Her path runs through KGRG, KEXP's Audioasis, the goth/industrial/wave crew Second Sight, Audiodrome at Pony, Mechanical Breakdown, Expansions, and countless local dance floors. As a selector, she brings hypnotic transitions, techno, synth, wave, and underground electronic music with a sharp ear for mood and history. Sharlese brings the real Seattle frequency: informed, dark-edged, community-deep, and never generic.

name: Sho Nuph
bio: Seattle DJ triple threat Shomari Shanks. In the Seattle music scene since 1989 at age 15. Known for soulful house music sets.

name: Shortstack
bio: Half of the ourHOUSE DJ duo alongside yourMOM, Justin "Shortstack" Fairley is a resident at Massive Club and Pony Seattle. As part of ourHOUSE, the duo brings a unique blend of classic house, disco, funk, and soul that brings people together. They hold residencies at Massive Club (1st Saturday ourHOUSE), Pony Seattle, and Cruise/Disco at the Seattle Eagle (2nd Saturday).

name: Shvili
bio: A music lover and selector from Tbilisi, Georgia. In the field of Music Therapy, classically trained pianist, with deep underground experience.Hailing from the vibrant city of Tbilisi, Georgia, Shvili has been immersed in the world of music from an early age. Growing up as a classical pianist, she honed her skills and earned several accolades showcasing her dedication to her craft. Her musical journey was enriched by singing in both chamber choirs and church choirs, further broadening her artistic horizons. Upon moving to the United States, Tatia attended an esteemed art school with a focus on music, where she continued to expand her musical repertoire. It was during these formative years that she discovered her profound connection with dance music. The pulsating beats of the underground scene resonated deeply within her, setting the stage for her future endeavors.This experience solidified her desire to make a name for herself in the bustling metropolis of NYC. Emerging as a pandemic artist, she utilized the time of societal isolation to perfect her art. With unwavering determination, she delved into the world of vinyl, crafting her unique sound and style. Her efforts have paid off, as she has had the honor of sharing the stage with respected artists such as Francesco del Garda, Anthea, Liquid Earth, Tini, Velasco, Nicola Cruz, Andrey Pushkarev, and many more. Known for her timeless records and playful spirit, Shvili infuses her sets with infectious grooves that captivate audiences. Her performances have graced some of the most prestigious clubs and events in the United States, including Public Records, ReSolute, Un_Mute, Flash DC, ATV Records, Apollo Studios, Jolene Sound Room, and more. With a rich musical background and a passion for creating unforgettable experiences, Tatia is poised to share her vibrant energy and distinctive sound with the world. Her journey is a testament to the power of music to transcend boundaries and connect souls on the dance floor.

name: Simone BG
bio: Seattle-based DJ and event producer born and raised on Capitol Hill. Known for dark, moody deep house and techno. Studio 4/4 resident. Plays Flammable, Shameless, and Viva events.

name: Simpa
bio: Simpa is a Seattle DJ with roots reaching back to 1993 and a deep connection to house and drum & bass culture. Public profiles frame Simpa as an electronic music veteran with a sharp ear for rhythm, bassline movement, and immersive energy. That long arc matters in a city where dance music history is carried by people who kept showing up before it was convenient.
